title: thymus serpyllum subsp. carniolicus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. carniolicus, also known as Alpine thyme or Carinthian thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of the Alps. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum subsp. carniolicus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and promote wound healing. Thymus serpyllum subsp. carniolicus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils for use in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
